source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - GI 47 AP Platform Incident

context.region: Gulf of America, Grand Isle Block 47

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Pressurized Gas Release
actions_taken: Injured person climbed down from working platform without assistance
Field paramedic flown from GI 43 platform to evaluate injured person
Injured person transported by helicopter to shore
Injured person transported by ambulance to Medical Center for evaluation
X-rays and CT scans performed at Medical Center
Re-evaluation at Urgent Care on 28 April 2025
GOM Shelf submitted incident report to BSEE on 08 May 2025
BSEE investigation conducted with collection of photographs, witness statements, and documentation
event.summary: On 25 April 2025 at approximately 1330 hours, a Louisiana Safety Systems, Inc. Valve Technician was performing maintenance on a control valve located on a 6-inch gas line at GI 47 AP platform. While disassembling the valve, a sudden pressurized gas release occurred, causing the valve bonnet flange assembly (approximately 50 pounds) to strike the technician in the left chest and abdomen area. The technician was knocked backwards onto an elevated scaffolding platform and possibly lost consciousness. The technician sustained a laceration behind the ear and an anterior rib fracture of the 8th left rib. The incident resulted from multiple failures including inadequate communication between the operator and contractor, failure to obtain required work permits and PIC approval, failure to verify zero pressure on the gas line through proper energy isolation procedures, use of unapproved scaffolding without fall protection, and non-adherence to manufacturer recommendations for valve disassembly.
recommendations: Management to meet with GI 47 AP leadership to review incident learnings and contributing factors
Site leadership must ensure tasks involving pressurized systems are authorized by PIC with appropriate documentation prior to work beginning
Third-party service personnel working on pressurized systems must be accompanied by operations group member to verify double-block and bleed and line bled-down verification
Scaffolding contractors to post red caution tape around unapproved scaffolding as additional visual indicator
Task JSA development to include collective discussion of all hazards and proper mitigation through clear communication
Task JSAs to include mitigation of maintaining four nuts on bolts to prevent flange dislodgement from unforeseen pressure releases
Management to develop and distribute Safety Alert describing case findings, learnings, and prevention actions
BSEE to consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
key_phrases: SCADA data showed 143 psi at time of release

hazards.name: Working at Heights on Elevated Platform

threats.name: Failure to Verify Zero Pressure on Gas Line
Lack of Work Authorization and Permits

consequences.name: Blunt Force Trauma to Chest and Abdomen

supporting_text: The IP verified that the 6-inch gas line's analog gauge indicated that there was zero pressure in the line
SCADA data taken from the Daniel's Meter on the 6-inch gas line upstream of the control valve indicated that there was 143 psi on the 6-inch gas line at the time of the gas release

controls.name: Energy Isolation Permit
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper isolation procedures
controls.lod_basis: Required procedural control before work authorization

supporting_text: the IP did not obtain the PIC's signature on the LSS JSA, the GOM Shelf Energy Isolation Permit to ensure the line was free of pressure

While there was an Energy Isolation Permit that was issued on 25 April 2025, GOM Shelf stated that the permit was issued for different valves that were being replaced, not the valve the IP was working on

controls.name: PIC Work Authorization and JSA Approval
controls.barrier_role: Supervisory control to ensure work is authorized and hazards are reviewed

supporting_text: the PIC of the platform never signed off on the JSA for the IP to begin work
the PIC admitted to signing the LSS Valve Technician's JSA after the incident occurred rather than before the job began
the IP did not receive permission to work at heights during the work being performed

controls.name: Needle Valve Pressure Relief
controls.barrier_role: Manual pressure relief to confirm zero pressure
controls.lod_basis: Secondary verification method for pressure confirmation

supporting_text: The IP then opened two needle valves, one on each side of the control valve, to allow any pressure that was in between the valves to be relieved
The IP stated that there were no signs of fluid or pressure coming out of the needle valves when opened

controls.name: Downstream Isolation Valve Lock-out
controls.barrier_role: Physical isolation to prevent backflow of pressure
controls.lod_basis: Primary isolation method for downstream section

supporting_text: the valve downstream of the control valve being worked on was closed and locked out
the downstream isolation valve was blocked off and locked out

controls.name: Upstream Isolation Verification
controls.barrier_role: Verification that upstream section is vented to atmosphere

supporting_text: the upstream side of the control valve being worked on was an open-ended piping which the IP took as further indication that the gas line was free of pressure
the upstream isolation valve having an open-end which indicated that it was vented to atmosphere and had no pressure on the 6-inch line
SCADA data taken from the Daniel's Meter on the 6-inch gas line upstream of the control valve indicated that there was 143 psi on the 6-inch gas line at the time of the gas release

controls.name: Manufacturer Recommended Valve Disassembly Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Technical control to prevent bonnet ejection during disassembly
controls.lod_basis: Manufacturer best practice for safe valve maintenance

supporting_text: The IP failed to follow manufacturer recommendations while working on the control valve. According to the GOM Shelf's internal investigation, it was stated that the IP failed to keep 4 nuts on the studs of the bonnet flange, which would prevent the possibility of the bonnet flange being forced upwards due to an unforeseen pressure release
the manufacturer recommends that during maintenance, the hex nuts or cap screws attaching the bonnet to the valve body should have been loosened approximately 3 mm or 1/8 inch

controls.name: Working at Heights Permit
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control for elevated work authorization
controls.lod_basis: Required permit for work above 6 feet

supporting_text: the IP did not obtain the PIC's signature on the LSS JSA, the GOM Shelf Energy Isolation Permit to ensure the line was free of pressure, or the Working at Heights Permit that is required when working above 6 feet of the main deck surface
Because the IP did not receive permission to work at heights during the work being performed, the IP used an unapproved scaffolding platform

controls.name: Fall Protection Equipment (Full Body Harness and Lifeline)
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to prevent injury from fall
controls.lod_basis: Required PPE for work at heights above 6 feet

supporting_text: The IP also failed to utilize the required fall-protection of a full body harness along with a retractable lifeline
the IP did not utilize the required fall protection while working 6 feet or higher from the main deck

controls.name: Approved Scaffolding with Safety Features
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to provide safe working platform
controls.lod_basis: Required safe work platform for elevated work

supporting_text: the IP used an unapproved scaffolding platform that was identified with a white tag by the Competent Scaffold Builder. The white tag indicated that the scaffolding was not safe to use as it was missing a ladder for entry and kick plates
The use of the scaffolding should have been addressed prior to the IP utilizing it to perform the maintenance on the control valve

SCADA data taken from the Daniel's Meter on the 6-inch gas line upstream of the control valve indicated that there was 143 psi on the 6-inch gas line at the time of the gas release which was at 1311 hours and 29 seconds
GOM Shelf stated that the SCADA could be slightly out of calibration, as it was still reading 15 psi after all the pressure was relieved

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) Development and Review
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify and mitigate hazards
controls.lod_basis: Required hazard identification and control planning

supporting_text: the BSEE AI found, on 25 April 2025, an LSS Valve Technician, the IP, completed a JSA for repairing a control valve on a 6-inch gas line located on GI 47 AP platform. In the JSA, the IP identified multiple hazards such as stored energy, pressure, pinch points, spills/leaks and tools/equipment
the IP's LSS Valve Technician JSA should have been reviewed by both the PIC of the platform and the IP before the job began. As the hazards were not discussed prior to commencing work

controls.name: Operator Verification of Line Bled Down
controls.barrier_role: Operator confirmation that pressure has been relieved
controls.lod_basis: Required operator verification before contractor work

supporting_text: By the IP failing to have the PIC verify energy isolation before commencing work, the trapped pressure in the 6-inch line went unidentified while the IP was performing maintenance on the control valve

the IP was told by the PIC that the 6-inch gas line had been bled down the morning of 25 April 2025. According to the PIC, the IP did not have his (the PIC's) authorization to begin the work on the control valve

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
